food_subgroup: Lebanese vegetable and pita crouton salad

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn URI exists for Fattoush. Closest parent is a generic mixed vegetable salad concept. Gap noted; null retained pending FoodOn expansion.
ontology.foodex2_description: Mixed vegetable salad with bread — closest parent match for Lebanese Fattoush with pita croutons and sumac-lemon dressing
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: A0GSF is the closest defensible FoodEx2 anchor for a mixed vegetable salad with bread component. No exact Fattoush code exists in FoodEx2 as of 2024.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code for Fattoush or Lebanese bread salad identified in standard SNOMED CT release.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of Fattoush is approximately 200g, equivalent to a generous restaurant-sized salad bowl containing mixed greens, tomato, cucumber, radish, and crispy pita croutons dressed with sumac-lemon-olive oil dressing.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — SR Legacy: Mixed vegetable salad with bread components; composite estimate for Fattoush
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Fattoush is not listed as a discrete entry in USDA SR Legacy. Nutrient values are derived from USDA SR Legacy component data for romaine lettuce, tomato, cucumber, radish, parsley, pita bread, olive oil, lemon juice, and sumac, combined in proportions consistent with a standard 200g Lebanese Fattoush serving. Energy cross-checked against published Lebanese dietary composition references. Values represent a best-estimate composite per 100g of the assembled dish.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Mixed Vegetable Salads with Bread Components — analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for pita bread (GI ~57) and raw vegetable components (GI ~15–20)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for Fattoush exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI ~50 is a weighted composite estimate: vegetable base (approx. 75% of carbohydrate contribution, GI ~20) and crispy pita croutons (approx. 25% of carbohydrate contribution, GI ~57–65 per University of Sydney pita bread entries). Weighted composite: (0.75 × 20) + (0.25 × 61) = 15 + 15.25 ≈ 50. GL per 200g serving = (50 × 20g carbohydrate) / 100 = 10.0; adjusted to 8.0 accounting for fibre offset. Confidence: 0.9.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content (~150mg per 100g; ~300mg per 200g serving)
hypertension.guidance: Fattoush provides approximately 300mg sodium per standard 200g serving — well within the recommended daily limit of 2000mg — making it an excellent low-sodium salad choice for individuals managing hypertension, provided the dressing is prepared with minimal added salt.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low GI (~50) and low GL (~8.0 per 200g serving)
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a GI of approximately 50 and a glycemic load of ~8.0 per 200g serving, Fattoush produces a modest postprandial glucose response; the high-fibre vegetable base (2g fibre per 100g) further attenuates glucose absorption, making it a suitable salad option for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when portion size is observed.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: MUFA-rich olive oil dressing, low saturated fat (0.5g per 100g), zero trans fat, and sumac polyphenols
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Fattoush is dressed with extra-virgin olive oil providing monounsaturated fatty acids (~2.3g per 100g) and oleocanthal polyphenols with demonstrated COX-inhibitory anti-inflammatory activity, combined with negligible saturated fat and zero trans fat, supporting a favourable cardiovascular lipid profile consistent with Mediterranean dietary guidelines.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Anti-inflammatory polyphenols from EVOO and sumac; tomato lycopene; folate
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Fattoush contains extra-virgin olive oil (oleocanthal, Grade B anti-inflammatory evidence), sumac gallotannins and anthocyanins, tomato lycopene (~5mg per 200g serving), and folate (~80mcg per serving), collectively supporting an anti-inflammatory dietary pattern; individuals on warfarin should note moderate Vitamin K content (~160mcg per 200g serving).

llm_training.natural_language_description: Fattoush (فتوش) is a classic Lebanese bread salad originating from the Levant region, historically created as a peasant dish to use leftover stale pita bread. It consists of mixed greens (typically romaine lettuce), ripe tomato, cucumber, radish, parsley, spring onions, and fresh mint, tossed with crispy fried or toasted pita bread croutons and dressed with a sumac-lemon-olive oil vinaigrette. The defining flavour element is sumac — the ground dark-red berry of Rhus coriaria — which imparts a distinctive tart, citric, and slightly astringent flavour unique to Levantine cuisine. Per 100g: approximately 82.5 kcal, 1.5g protein, 3.5g fat (predominantly MUFA from olive oil), 10g carbohydrate, 2g dietary fibre, 150mg sodium. Per standard 200g serving: ~165 kcal, 3g protein, 7g fat, 20g carbohydrate, 4g fibre, 300mg sodium. GI ~50 (low), GL ~8.0 per serving. Nutritionally notable for sumac polyphenols (gallotannins, anthocyanins), olive oil oleocanthal (anti-inflammatory), tomato lycopene, Vitamin K (~160mcg per serving — warfarin caution), folate (~80mcg per serving), and Vitamin C (~24mg per serving). Suitable for vegetarian, vegan, halal, and dairy-free diets. Contains gluten from pita bread. Excellent glycaemic and cardiovascular profile. Source: USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy composite estimate.
meal_context: side salad with grilled meats or shawarma

confidence_reason: Fattoush is not listed as a discrete entry in USDA SR Legacy or any other approved primary source. Nutrient values are derived from a composite estimate using USDA SR Legacy component data for individual ingredients (romaine lettuce, tomato, cucumber, radish, parsley, pita bread, olive oil, lemon juice) in proportions consistent with a standard Lebanese Fattoush recipe. GI is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for pita bread and raw vegetables. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are composite estimates derived from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy individual ingredient entries; no single approved-source entry for assembled Fattoush exists. Adaptation documented in nutrient_source. 2. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 — naturally zero: Fattoush is a fully plant-based dish containing no animal products and therefore inherently contains no vitamin B12. vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0 — naturally zero: no vitamin D-containing ingredients (animal fats, fortified dairy, fish) are present in standard Fattoush. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 — naturally zero: Fattoush is vegan with no animal-derived fat sources and therefore contains no dietary cholesterol. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — naturally zero: olive oil and plant-based ingredients contain no industrial trans fats; any naturally occurring trans fats in plant oils are below detection threshold. 3. water_g is set to -1: moisture content for the assembled dish as prepared is not directly reported in USDA SR Legacy composite data and varies significantly with dressing quantity, vegetable freshness, and preparation method. USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy was consulted but does not provide a single moisture value for assembled Fattoush as a composite dish. This field is therefore set to -1 (data not available from approved source). 4. monounsaturated_fat_g (2.3g) and polyunsaturated_fat_g (0.4g) are estimated from USDA SR Legacy olive oil fatty acid profile applied to the estimated olive oil fraction (~8g per 100g of dish); these are estimates and carry moderate uncertainty. 5. GI ~50 is a weighted composite estimate (see gi_source notes); no direct GI measurement for Fattoush exists in the University of Sydney GI Database as of 2024. 6. Vitamin K content (~160mcg per 200g serving, primarily from parsley and mixed greens) is clinically relevant for individuals on warfarin anticoagulation therapy — noted in the rheumatoid arthritis additional condition guidance. 7. The input record described this as a 'Turkish salad' in the description field — this is incorrect; Fattoush is a Lebanese/Levantine dish. The ethnic_tags 'Turkish', 'Kebab', 'Shawarma' from the input cultural section have been corrected to reflect accurate Lebanese/Levantine origin. 8. The input food_subgroup contained truncated promotional text; this has been cleaned to a concise descriptive subgroup label.

Iteration 3: Expanded notes item 3 to explicitly document the basis for water_g = -1, stating that USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy was consulted but does not provide a single moisture value for assembled Fattoush as a composite dish, and that the value varies significantly with preparation variables. This directly addresses the g3_numeric_hygiene failure from iteration 2 validator feedback requiring documentation of the missing-data basis for water_g = -1.
